summary.current_state: coderef-core writes relative file paths into .coderef/index.json, but the drift generator compares raw current scan elements that still carry absolute file paths. Because element identity is file:name:line, the same unchanged element fails to match between scans.
summary.target_state: Drift detection should normalize both current and previous elements to the same project-relative path format before computing added, deleted, modified, and driftPercentage, so unchanged rescans report 0% drift.
summary.user_visible_failure: coderef-workflow strict planning currently hard-fails on a fresh populate because .coderef/reports/drift.json reports 200% drift with every element appearing both added and deleted.

key_files.reason: Owns drift detection, element identity, and the report output that currently mixes absolute and relative paths.
Needs a regression test that proves unchanged elements do not drift when previous index paths are relative and current state paths are absolute.
Defines the canonical relative path format already written into index.json and should remain the normalization target.
Built output must reflect the source fix after the TypeScript build.

root_cause.mismatch: previous index elements use relative file paths while current pipeline state elements use absolute file paths
root_cause.identity_rule: drift-generator element IDs are built as file:name:line
root_cause.effect: all unchanged elements miss identity matching and are counted as both added and deleted

gaps_and_risks: If only the drift percentage is patched in downstream preflight, drift.json will remain incorrect and other consumers will still see false drift.
If only current elements are normalized and report payloads still emit absolute file paths, downstream artifacts will stay inconsistent.
If the regression test does not cover absolute-versus-relative identity matching, the bug can recur during future scanner refactors.

recommended_change.workflow: Normalize both current and previous elements to the same project-relative path format before map construction and before writing added, deleted, and modified report entries.
tests: Add a regression case where previous index uses relative paths and the current state uses absolute paths for the same unchanged element, expecting 0% drift.
Keep the existing added/deleted/modified test intact so real drift still registers correctly.
recommended_change.validation: Rebuild coderef-core, repopulate coderef-workflow, and confirm strict preflight no longer fails on false 200% drift.
